context: Cloud infrastructure SaaS with usage-based pricing. 200 enterprise customers. NRR 128%. Monthly churn 1.5% but expansion from usage growth compensates 3x.
diagnosis: Elite revenue engine. Dynamic LTV is 1.8x higher than static LTV because expansion compounds. Architecture must scale cheaply — cloud cost per transaction IS the margin. Protect the flywheel.
applicability: Usage-based B2B SaaS with strong product-market fit

context: B2B SaaS startup. 500 customers. 60% of churn happens in first 45 days. ARPU €199/month. Monthly churn 7% blended, but 15% in month 1 and 3% after month 3.
diagnosis: Classic onboarding failure. Static LTV using blended churn is misleadingly low. The real LTV for retained customers (past month 3) is significantly higher. Fix activation, not product. 60% of churn is preventable.
applicability: Early-stage B2B SaaS with activation problems

context: Mid-market SaaS with tiered pricing but <5% upgrade rate. 1000 customers. NRR 97%. Churn is low (2%/month) but zero expansion. Every cohort flatlines.
diagnosis: The product retains but doesn't grow. Feature gap between tiers is cosmetic — customers don't see a reason to upgrade. Need real 'unlock' features tied to business outcomes. Missing €420k/year in expansion revenue.
applicability: Mid-market SaaS with good retention but no expansion mechanics

context: Collaboration tool sold seat-based to enterprises. Average deal: 50 seats purchased. Only 12 seats active (24% utilization). NRR 92% — customers downsizing at renewal.
diagnosis: Seat-based model failing because internal adoption never happened. 60% of revenue at risk of downsizing. Product isn't indispensable to teams — only the buyer uses it. Need team collaboration features and admin ROI dashboards immediately.
applicability: Seat-based B2B with low internal adoption
